item.title: Quantum dots reveal hidden light waves on metal surfaces
item.description: Photographs can reveal things that are otherwise impossible for the naked eye to see, be they distant galaxies or microscopic cells. Researchers at Osaka Metropolitan University have developed a practical and versatile imaging technique that makes another usually invisible phenomenon visible: surface plasmon polaritons (SPPs), light waves that travel along metal surfaces.

item.title: Growing Better Hemp Grain: Rodale Institute Studies How Cover Crops Boost Yield and Nutrition - Rodale Institute
item.description: INDUSTRIAL HEMP IS RE-EMERGING AS A VALUABLE CROP. Since the 2018 Farm Bill opened the door to growing it as a mainstream agricultural commodity, farmers

item.title: 2022 IEEE President K.J. Ray Liu Honored for His Leadership
item.description: He led the effort for more equitable representation of members

item.title: NASA’s ESCAPADE Snaps Family Portrait of Earth, Moon
item.description: On July 3, 2026, NASA’s Mars-destined ESCAPADE spacecraft captured photos of Earth and the Moon in visible and thermal infrared light.

item.title: Algorithms create foundry-ready photonic circuits
item.description: Photonic microchips can process data at extremely high speeds and are embedded in a wide variety of today's technologies. Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for the Science of Light (MPL) and Harvard University have now succeeded in developing three functional components for such chips that are up to 500 times smaller than conventional designs. The researchers used inverse design, a computer algorithm, to achieve this. The results are published in Nature Communications.

item.title: Watch the teaser trailer for 'Blade Runner 2099,' a new sci-fi series coming to Prime Video November 25
item.description: The limited series starring Michelle Yeoh and Hunter Schafer is set 50 years after 'Blade Runner 2049'.

item.description: Here is a complete list of all Fortnite Kicks!

item.title: ModelExpress: Distributing Model Artifacts at the Speed of Light | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Every byte moved has a cost. As model checkpoints grow to hundreds of gigabytes or even a terabyte, that cost adds up quickly. To make things even worse, moving these model weights around the cluster…

item.title: New Small Spacecraft Technology Demonstration Mission at the Moon
item.description: NASA is working with industry to advance the next phase of cislunar infrastructure for the agency’s Artemis program and Moon Base, including orbital assets

item.title: Metal-ion coating improves extracellular vesicles for cancer detection and targeted delivery
item.description: Researchers, including those from the University of Tokyo, found a way to optimize how cells bind to small packages they release called extracellular vesicles. By coating the vesicles with metal ions, they made cells and their corresponding vesicles stick together more strongly than they would naturally. This reduced the time needed for cells to capture their own vesicles, even in mixtures containing billions of other vesicles.

item.title: HWO SIG Seminar, 12 August 2026 - NASA Science
item.description: Habitable Worlds Observatory SIG Seminar. Details TBA

item.title: Community Science (Ad ASTRA) Workshop, Sept 2026 - NASA Science
item.description: 1-3 September 2026. The Community Science (Ad ASTRA) Workshop is organized by the NASA Astrophysics Division to engage the community in defining future large, strategic astrophysics missions.

item.title: G2 Esports' $35M business blueprint: How the org profits whether fans love or hate them
item.description: G2 CEO Alban Dechelotte pulled back the curtain on the org's commercial engine at the Esports World Cup 2026 in Paris, revealing a $35M annual turnover built on diversified revenue and a deliberately borderless identity.

item.title: AI vs expert: Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini take on our LEC week one predictions
item.description: Claude, ChatGPT and Gemini each built a $10 LEC week one accumulator — see how their picks and odds stack up against our own expert's predictions.

item.title: Hubble Spies One-Sided Spiral - NASA Science
item.description: This Hubble image features the spiral galaxy NGC 4654, located 72 million light-years away in the constellation Virgo (the Maiden).

item.title: EMBL spin-off Epitome Therapeutics launches to advance precision epigenome editing | EMBL
item.description: EMBL spin-off Epitome Therapeutics will develop a new generation of precision medicines using epigenome editing to precisely regulate gene expression.

item.title: At AI Summit, South Korea Outlines Its AI Future With NVIDIA and Partners
item.description: At this week’s AI Summit in San Francisco, South Korean President Jae Myung Lee and some of the country’s top business leaders and researchers are meeting with NVIDIA and ecosystem partners to chart Korea’s AI progress.

item.title: A Million-Panel Project - NASA Science
item.description: Utah’s new solar power and battery storage facility is among the largest in the region.

item.description: Bi-weekly information on upcoming and recently released multimedia, publications, data, products, and reports.

item.title: EPRI Journal: Celebrating Fifty Years of the Long View - EPRI Journal
item.description: Share this article:By Chris Warren EPRI had a very clear mission when it was founded in 1972. Launched in response to a 1965 blackout that left around 30 million people in eight Northeastern states and two Canadian provinces without power, the formation of EPRI was an acknowledgment both of the vital importance of electricity and […]

item.title: Riot suspends NAVI streamer ardiis for transphobic Game Changers comments
item.description: After transphobic comments, ardiis will be unable to co-stream VCT matches for one month, but many fans believe the punishment isn't enough.

item.title: Editors of economics journal resign after association renews Taylor & Francis contract
item.description: Forty-one editors, associate editors and advisory board members resigned from their roles at the Review of Social Economy after the Association for Social Economics, which owns the journal, renewed…

item.title: First 3D nanoscale maps of human airway epithelium reveal hidden architecture of lung defense cells
item.description: Researchers at Queen Mary University of London and University of Cambridge in collaboration with colleagues at HHMI Janelia and NIH, have created some of the most detailed three-dimensional maps ever generated of human airway cells, revealing how the specialized cells that protect our lungs are built and organized.

item.title: Nanopores can activate human T cells without biochemical signals
item.description: Immune cells protect and heal the human body. In medicine, they are specifically activated through biochemical reactions to treat certain diseases. Researchers at the Helmholtz-Zentrum Hereon, ETH Zurich, Humboldt University of Berlin, Charité Berlin and Inselspital Bern have now discovered that immune cells also respond to the surface structure of materials—without chemistry.

item.title: RORA Coach Midnight on facing TLPH early in MSC at EWC 2026: "Fortunate and unfortunate at the same time."
item.description: RORA Coach Midnight talks about the Rafaela pick and how facing TLPH early in the MSC at EWC 2026 was both "fortunate and unfortunate."

item.title: Exclusive: A couple paid more than $800,000 for a gene-editing therapy for their daughter. She died, and it wasn’t made public
item.description: Illustration: Daria Lada An investigation by Science and Retraction Watch has uncovered details about a clinical trial that resulted in the death of its sole patient: a 6-year-old girl with a rare …

item.title: What Americans think about the global AI race
item.description: Most Americans see China as more advanced than the U.S. in AI development, while 43% say U.S. leadership in AI is extremely or very important.

item.title: As the 2026 Midterms Approach, Economy Is Front and Center
item.description: Americans are now almost evenly split over which party they most agree with on economic policies: 37% say the Democrats, while 36% say the GOP.

It’s time for soundproof booths to return to esports
item.description: After a Call of Duty Grand Final was heavily influenced by crowd behavior, we need to bring back soundproof booths in esports.

item.title: Debugging Ray Tracing Applications Using NVIDIA OptiX Toolkit | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: NVIDIA OptiX ray tracing engine is an application framework for achieving optimal ray tracing performance on the GPU. Applications using OptiX can fail in ways that are difficult to diagnose: an…

item.title: "Keep fighting for your dream": Team Vitality MLBB pro Vivian on overcoming challenges and judgement to become a top esports pro
item.description: Team Vitality Mobile Legends: Bang Bang (MLBB) player Vivi "Vivian" Indrawaty changed her life and her family's by banking on her esports career.

item.title: Lipid nanoparticles deliver one-two punch to shrink resistant oral tumors in preclinical models
item.description: Oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC) is the most common form of head and neck cancer, and the number of new cases is predicted to rise by 30% in the next decade. Despite decades of cancer research, treating OSCC remains a challenge. The five-year survival rate is approximately 50%, and standard treatments such as ablative surgery and radiation often leave patients with permanent disfigurement or profound, lifelong impairment of essential oral functions such as speaking and swallowing.

item.title: Start Customizing NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano with Prime Intellect Lab in Minutes | NVIDIA Technical Blog
item.description: Customization is what enables developers to take a general model and tailor it to use cases, domains, languages, and more. However, customization comes with a few challenges.

item.title: NASA’s MAVEN Illuminates New Understanding of Auroras at Mars - NASA Science
item.description: NASA MAVEN (Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution) mission scientists have uncovered a key puzzle piece in understanding certain types of auroras on Mars,

item.title: PlayVS takes esports to 50,000 Scouts at the 2026 National Jamboree
item.description: PlayVS is rolling out a dedicated gaming arena at Scouting America's biggest youth gathering, running July 22–31 in West Virginia — part of a broader push to plant esports in unexpected spaces.

item.title: Combining AI with laboratory evolution yields improved proteins
item.description: Researchers found that AI-redesigned versions of natural proteins serve as superior starting points to evolve proteins with new functions.

item.description: Plus, ‘Battlefield 6’ updates, Capcom classics, ‘Halo: Campaign Evolved Advanced Access’ and nine new games arrive on the cloud this week.

item.title: New NERC-funded project empowers young people to engage with environmental science - British Geological Survey
item.description: BGS is part of a new, collaborative project, funded through NERC’s ‘Opening up the environment’ programme, helping the environmental research community become more accessible.

item.title: Hans Jagnow on Paris, politics, and building fandom across 24 games at the Esports World Cup
item.description: Esports Foundation's Hans Jagnow talks about the Esports World Cup's relocation, the changing perception of esports, and esports fandom.

item.description: Track the LCQ action for Street Fighter 6 EWC 2026 here! Watch the stream, check out the top participants, and discover characters in play.

item.title: Risk without reward: Counter-Strike 2's new sticker system threatens the entire esports scene's structure
item.description: Valve just made some big changes to the sticker system, causing Counter-Strike 2 teams to lose a ton of revenue. This could spell trouble.

item.title: New Data Tables Released From Household Trends and Outlook Pulse Survey
item.description: New data tables released about employment, social connectedness, and other topics from the experimental HTOPS.
